openmeter/billing/charges/worker/advance.purpose: Batch auto-advance worker that iterates all customers with eligible charges and calls charges.Service.AdvanceCharges for each; errors are collected and joined rather than short-circuiting, so one failing customer does not block others.

patterns.name: Fan-out with error accumulation
patterns.description: Loop over all customers, accumulate errors via `errs = append(errs, ...)`, return `errors.Join(errs...)` so all failures are reported and processing continues.
patterns.example: for _, cust := range customers { if err := a.AdvanceCharges(ctx, cust); err != nil { errs = append(errs, err) } }; return errors.Join(errs...)

patterns.name: Pagination via CollectAll
patterns.description: Use `pagination.CollectAll` with a `pagination.NewPaginator` closure to fetch all pages of customers without manual page-loop code.
patterns.example: pagination.CollectAll(ctx, pagination.NewPaginator(func(ctx context.Context, page pagination.Page) (pagination.Result[customer.CustomerID], error) { return a.chargesService.ListCustomersToAdvance(ctx, ...) }), defaultPageSize)

patterns.name: Config struct + constructor validation
patterns.description: All dependencies passed via a `Config` struct; `NewAdvancer` validates non-nil fields before constructing the struct.
patterns.example: func NewAdvancer(config Config) (*AutoAdvancer, error) { if config.ChargesService == nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf(...) } ... }

patterns.name: Advance via service interface only
patterns.description: All charge advancement is driven through `charges.ChargeService.AdvanceCharges` — never call adapters or Ent directly from this layer.

patterns.name: Structured logging with slog
patterns.description: Use `slog.InfoContext`/`DebugContext`/`ErrorContext`/`WarnContext` with named key-value pairs (`slog.String`) for all log statements.
patterns.example: a.logger.ErrorContext(ctx, "failed to auto-advance charges", slog.String("namespace", ...), slog.String("customer_id", ...))

key_file_guides.role: Sole file; defines AutoAdvancer struct, All/ListCustomersToAdvance/AdvanceCharges methods, Config struct, and NewAdvancer constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: defaultPageSize=10_000 caps single-page fetches; `now` is captured once at the start of ListCustomersToAdvance to give a stable cutoff for the entire batch run.

anti_patterns: Returning on first customer error — must accumulate and join errors
Calling Ent/adapter code directly instead of going through charges.ChargeService
Introducing context.Background() — always propagate the caller's ctx
Hard-coding namespaces instead of accepting them as a parameter

decisions.decision: Error accumulation instead of early return
decisions.rationale: A single customer failure should not prevent advancing charges for all other customers in the batch.

decisions.decision: Time captured once per All() call
decisions.rationale: Using a single `now` for the entire batch ensures consistent cutoff semantics; per-customer clock calls could admit races where newly eligible charges slip into or out of the window mid-run.

openmeter/billing/charges/worker/asyncad.purpose: Event-driven (async) handler that advances charges for a single customer in response to a `charges.AdvanceChargesEvent`; intended to be wired as a Watermill/event-bus message handler.

patterns.name: Config.Validate() before construction
patterns.description: Config struct carries a `Validate() error` method that checks all required fields; `New` calls it before constructing the Handler.
patterns.example: func New(c Config) (*Handler, error) { if err := c.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } ... }

patterns.name: Single-responsibility Handle method
patterns.description: Handler exposes exactly one `Handle(ctx, *AdvanceChargesEvent) error` method; no batch logic, no pagination — those live in the advance package.
patterns.example: func (h *Handler) Handle(ctx context.Context, event *charges.AdvanceChargesEvent) error { _, err := h.chargesService.AdvanceCharges(ctx, ...) }

patterns.name: CustomerID constructed from event fields
patterns.description: Build `customer.CustomerID{Namespace: event.Namespace, ID: event.CustomerID}` inline — no helper, no global state.

patterns.name: Warn-then-return on service error
patterns.description: Log a WarnContext entry with namespace/customer_id/error keys before returning the error so the upstream router can decide on retry.
patterns.example: h.logger.WarnContext(ctx, "failed to advance charges", slog.String("namespace", ...), slog.String("error", err.Error())); return err

key_file_guides.role: Sole file; defines Handler, Config (with Validate), New constructor, and Handle method.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Handler does not discard errors — it returns them so the message bus can apply retry/dead-letter logic. Never swallow the error here.

anti_patterns: Adding batch/pagination logic here — that belongs in the advance package
Calling Ent or adapter code directly instead of charges.ChargeService
Returning nil on service error to suppress retries
Introducing context.Background() instead of propagating the event's ctx

decisions.rationale: Async (event-driven, single-customer) and sync (batch, all-customers) advancement have different error-handling and retry contracts; keeping them in separate packages avoids conflating them.
